Findings & Stories

The leaderboard is the table. These are the reasons behind the table.

Insights turns raw Elo movement into benchmark lessons: what failed, what improved, and which scoring choices changed conclusions.

Findings

5

launch stories

Core risk

URLs

fabricated or weak citations

Lens

Elo

pairwise, not average-only

Output

Paper

NeurIPS-ready narrative

F6

Fluent hallucination beats naive judges

The prettiest report can still fabricate unreachable URLs. Composite v3.1 makes citation reachability a first-class ranking signal.

Read methodology context
Dual judge

Judge family changes the ordering

A same-family judge inflates familiar answer styles. The benchmark separates agent and judge families to reduce self-preference.

Read methodology context
+162 Elo

Adapter quality can beat framework reputation

DeerFlow moved sharply after shim and backend fixes, showing that integration quality is part of real-world agent performance.

Read methodology context
RACE

Long answers are not necessarily grounded answers

LLM judges often reward coverage and polish. URL-level verifiers reveal when length hides unsupported claims.

Read methodology context
Pareto

Only a few agents are cost-quality efficient

The efficient frontier is small: most agents are dominated once quality, cost, and dropped runs are considered together.

Read methodology context

Deep Research
Arena

The first reproducible Elo benchmark for Deep Research agents. Open source. Open data. Open methodology.
